query: Virgin Galactic (SPCE) financing, capital raise, cash position and going concern status in 2026. Has the company raised significant capital or secured funding in Q2 2026 (April-June 2026)? What is their current cash runway?
answer:
Research complete. Here's the summary of my findings on Virgin Galactic (SPCE) financing and going concern status:

## Key Findings

**Going Concern:** Virgin Galactic's Q1 2026 10-Q (filed May 14, 2026) **explicitly disclosed substantial doubt about its ability to continue as a going concern**, contingent on raising new funding or achieving a successful commercial launch.

**Cash & Runway:**
- ~$251 million in cash, equivalents and marketable securities at March 31, 2026
- Cash burn of ~$90M/quarter (Q1 FCF –$93.3M; Q2 guidance –$87M to –$92M)
- Implied runway of ~2.5–3 quarters (into early 2027); Simply Wall St called it "less than 1 year"

**Q2 2026 (April–June) capital raise — the direct question:**
- **No large discrete capital raise appears to have closed specifically in Q2 2026.** Financing has been incremental rather than a headline injection.
- The company relies on an **ATM equity program with Jefferies** (~$87M remaining at Q1; capacity later restored to $45.59M) and **debt-to-equity conversions** (~$30.5M of 9.80% notes converted to equity in 2026).
- The major restructuring occurred earlier — the **December 2025 capital realignment** that repurchased ~$354.6M of 2027 convertible notes, cut principal from $425M to ~$273M, and pushed maturity to Dec 2028.

**Important caveat:** Q2 2026 results (ended June 30) were not yet reported as of July 9, 2026, so the current post-June cash balance isn't disclosed. Notably, SPCE spiked to $7.52 on June 1 during a meme rally (from ~$2.33 in late April, back to ~$2.70 by July 9) — that window would have materially boosted ATM raise capacity, but any Q2 ATM sales aren't yet quantified in filings.

The full synthesis and structured data (17 data points, 12 sources) have been recorded.
